prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: parents, staff, students
Channel scope: policy-documents, staff-handbook, parent-handbook

Topic document:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Jewellery must not dangle; no hoop earrings or large necklaces (health and safety). On PE days, students must be able to remove stud earrings or other items as required.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Subtle make-up may be worn from Year 7 upwards (e.g., mascara, powder, foundation, nail varnish); no lipstick.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Acrylic nails may not be longer than two millimetres from the fingertip (health and safety).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Shorts and skirts must cover the upper thighs (¾ length or longer). Cycling shorts are permitted on PE days but must also cover the upper thighs.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

All clothes must be without holes or rips. All underwear must be covered by outerwear.

On physical education (PE) days, students must be able to remove stud earrings or other items as needed.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","student-dress-code"]},{"title":"Make-Up Guidelines","content":"Students from Year 7 onwards may wear subtle make-up, such as mascara, powder, foundation, and nail varnish. Lipstick is not allowed.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","student-dress-code"]},{"title":"Acrylic Nail Length","content":"Students' acrylic nails must not extend more than two millimetres from the fingertip, for health and safety reasons.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","student-dress-code"]},{"title":"Shorts and Skirts Length","content":"Students' shorts and skirts must cover the upper thighs, requiring them to be ¾ length or longer. On PE days, cycling shorts are allowed but must also adhere to this length requirement.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","student-dress-code"]},{"title":"Clothing Condition","content":"Students must wear clothes without holes or rips and ensure that underwear is covered by outerwear at all times.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","student-dress-code"]}]}

facts.title: Jewellery Restrictions
facts.content: Students must not wear jewellery that dangles, including hoop earrings and large necklaces, to ensure health and safety. On physical education (PE) days, students must be able to remove stud earrings or other items as needed.

facts.title: Make-Up Guidelines
facts.content: Students from Year 7 onwards may wear subtle make-up, such as mascara, powder, foundation, and nail varnish. Lipstick is not allowed.

facts.title: Acrylic Nail Length
facts.content: Students' acrylic nails must not extend more than two millimetres from the fingertip, for health and safety reasons.

facts.title: Shorts and Skirts Length
facts.content: Students' shorts and skirts must cover the upper thighs, requiring them to be ¾ length or longer. On PE days, cycling shorts are allowed but must also adhere to this length requirement.

facts.title: Clothing Condition
facts.content: Students must wear clothes without holes or rips and ensure that underwear is covered by outerwear at all times.
